title: Reranker — what it is, how it works, and a live in-browser demo | reranker.uk
description: A practical guide to rerankers for search and RAG: what a rerank model is, cross-encoder vs bi-encoder, how to add reranking to RAG, model comparisons (bge, Cohere, Jina, Voyage), and a free demo that runs entirely in your browser.
og_title: Reranker — the resource + a live in-browser rerank demo
og_description: Learn rerankers for RAG and search, compare rerank models, and try a cross-encoder reranking demo that runs 100% in your browser — no API, no cost.

        "description": "A resource on rerankers for retrieval and RAG, plus a browser-based reranking demo."
      },
      {
        "@type": "FAQPage",
        "mainEntity": [
          {
            "@type": "Question",
            "name": "What is a reranker?",
            "acceptedAnswer": { "@type": "Answer", "text": "A reranker is a model that takes a query and a set of candidate documents and reorders them by relevance. It usually runs as a second stage after fast retrieval, scoring each query–document pair jointly with a cross-encoder for higher precision." }
          },
          {
            "@type": "Question",
            "name": "Why use a rerank model in RAG?",
            "acceptedAnswer": { "@type": "Answer", "text": "Vector search returns roughly relevant chunks, but the order is often imperfect. A rerank model re-scores the top candidates so the most relevant passages land at the top of the prompt, improving answer quality and letting you send fewer tokens to the LLM." }
          }
        ]
      }
    ]
  }
